offers.eyebrow: For universities, colleges and research offices
offers.title: Institutional Research Integrity
offers.metaDescription: Research-integrity diagnostics, policy and workflow review, publication governance and evidence controls for universities, colleges and research offices.
offers.intro: Build a research system that can be explained, evidenced and defended — from journal selection and authorship to data handling, ethics and corrections.
offers.problem: Research integrity often fails through disconnected routines rather than one dramatic breach: unclear authorship rules, weak journal checks, informal data handling, inconsistent AI use and no auditable correction path.
offers.inaction: Left unresolved, those gaps can produce avoidable retractions, disputed authorship, unreliable institutional reporting, reputational harm and repeated remediation work.
for: Universities and colleges strengthening research governance
Research offices, ethics committees, libraries and IQAC teams
Institutions preparing policy, training or audit-ready evidence controls
notFor: Teams seeking a cosmetic policy without operational change
Anyone asking us to conceal misconduct or manufacture records
Individuals seeking ghostwriting, authorship placement or guaranteed publication
outcomes: A documented picture of current controls and priority gaps
A practical integrity framework aligned to the institution's actual workflows
Clear ownership, evidence requirements and escalation routes
A sequenced implementation plan that can be reviewed internally
deliverables: Research-integrity diagnostic and risk register
Policy and workflow gap analysis
Journal-verification, authorship, AI-use and correction controls
Evidence checklist, responsibility map and 90-day action plan
Closing briefing for the responsible institutional team
inputs: Current policies, forms and committee terms of reference
A sample of research, publication and ethics workflows
Named process owners and known recurring problem areas
Access to relevant non-sensitive records in an agreed format

method.title: Map
method.text: Identify the decision points, records and people involved in the current research lifecycle.

method.title: Test
method.text: Review controls against visible evidence, not policy wording alone.

method.title: Prioritise
method.text: Separate immediate integrity risks from longer-term capability improvements.

method.title: Embed
method.text: Deliver usable controls, ownership and a review rhythm rather than a static report.

entryOffer.name: Research Integrity Diagnostic
entryOffer.scope: A fixed first phase covering policy and workflow review, a prioritised risk register and an implementation briefing.
entryOffer.timeline: Typically 1–3 weeks after complete inputs and access are confirmed.
entryOffer.investment: Starts at ₹1,00,000 for institution-wide advisory. Focused research-design reviews may fall within the published ₹15,000–₹1,00,000 research-consulting range.

priceFactors: Number of faculties, centres or workflows in scope
Volume and condition of existing policies and records
Whether new policy drafting, training or implementation support is required
On-site requirements; remote delivery is the default unless agreed otherwise
exclusions: No concealment, alteration or retrospective manufacture of evidence
No legal opinion, disciplinary adjudication or forensic investigation
No guaranteed accreditation, publication or regulatory outcome
No use of confidential client material in public AI systems

faq.a: No. It is a governance and control review. Suspected misconduct requiring legal, disciplinary or forensic handling should be referred to appropriately authorised specialists.

faq.q: Can you write our research-integrity policy?
faq.a: Policy drafting can be included after the diagnostic, but it must reflect real ownership, workflows and evidence. We do not provide a generic policy presented as implementation.

faq.q: Will you train faculty and research staff?
faq.a: Training can be scoped as a second phase and tied to the control gaps identified in the diagnostic.

offers.subject: Institutional Research Integrity Diagnostic

offers.serviceType: NAAC and IQAC Evidence Readiness Advisory
offers.eyebrow: For higher-education institutions and IQAC teams
offers.title: NAAC/IQAC Evidence Readiness
offers.metaTitle: NAAC and IQAC Evidence Readiness Advisory | Gurjas
offers.metaDescription: Criterion-wise NAAC evidence readiness, documentation diagnostics, data-quality review and IQAC advisory for universities and colleges.
offers.intro: Move from scattered documents to criterion-wise, traceable evidence — without paperwork theatre, manufactured claims or last-minute reconstruction.
offers.problem: Institutions often have substantial activity but cannot retrieve consistent, criterion-wise evidence or explain how figures were produced. Gaps surface late, when the cost of correction is highest.
offers.inaction: Unresolved evidence gaps can create contradictory submissions, weak verification trails, avoidable DVV questions, staff overload and a fragile accreditation process.
for: Universities and colleges approaching a NAAC cycle
IQAC teams strengthening evidence architecture before submission pressure
Institutional leaders who want a truthful readiness picture and clear ownership
notFor: Institutions seeking fabricated evidence or inflated claims
Teams unwilling to disclose material gaps to responsible leadership
Organisations expecting a guaranteed grade or outcome
outcomes: A criterion-wise view of evidence readiness and material gaps
A traceable structure connecting claims, source records and owners
Prioritised remediation before full drafting or verification
A realistic decision on whether the institution is ready for the next stage
deliverables: Criterion-wise evidence inventory and gap analysis
Source-to-claim traceability matrix
Data-quality and consistency review
DVV/DCF readiness checklist and ownership map
Leadership briefing with sequenced remediation priorities
inputs: Current accreditation stage, timetable and institutional profile
Available criterion-wise records, policies and data extracts
Access to IQAC and criterion owners for structured clarification
Known data-quality, retrieval or verification concerns

method.title: Inventory
method.text: Map what exists, where it sits and who can evidence it.

method.title: Trace
method.text: Connect each material claim to a source, calculation and responsible owner.

method.title: Challenge
method.text: Test consistency, completeness and likely verification questions.

method.title: Ready
method.text: Prioritise remediation and define the next defensible stage of work.

entryOffer.name: NAAC Evidence Readiness Diagnostic
entryOffer.scope: A fixed first phase covering criterion-wise inventory, gap analysis, traceability and a leadership readiness briefing — without SSR drafting.
entryOffer.timeline: The initial diagnostic is typically planned over 1–3 weeks once a complete document set and process owners are available.
entryOffer.investment: ₹1,00,000–₹2,50,000 for documentation and readiness advisory. Full SSR preparation and peer-visit support, when separately agreed, is typically ₹2,50,000–₹6,00,000.

priceFactors: Institution type, accreditation stage and number of criteria in scope
Document volume, data quality and retrieval effort
Whether the engagement stops at diagnosis or includes drafting and mock review
exclusions: No fabricated, backdated or misleading evidence
No guaranteed grade, accreditation decision or peer-team response
No submission of figures that the institution cannot source and explain
No use of confidential institutional material outside the written scope

faq.q: Does the diagnostic include SSR drafting?
faq.a: No. The entry diagnostic establishes readiness, traceability and priorities. Full SSR preparation is a separate scope and only uses evidence supplied and validated by the institution.

faq.q: Can you guarantee a NAAC grade?
faq.a: No. We improve evidence organisation, methodological clarity and readiness; accreditation decisions remain entirely with the authorised body.

faq.q: Can the work be delivered remotely?
faq.a: Yes, where records and responsible staff are available securely online. Any on-site requirement is agreed explicitly in the written scope.

offers.subject: NAAC Evidence Readiness Diagnostic

offers.eyebrow: For NGOs, CSR teams, foundations and policy programmes
offers.title: Impact Evaluation and Analytics
offers.metaTitle: Impact Evaluation and Analytics for NGO and CSR Programmes | Gurjas
offers.metaDescription: Evaluation design, indicator frameworks, quantitative and qualitative analysis, triangulation and decision-ready impact reporting for NGO, CSR and policy programmes.
offers.intro: Turn programme activity and field data into a defensible evaluation design, transparent analysis and decision-ready evidence.
offers.problem: Programme teams are often asked to prove impact after delivery has begun, with inconsistent indicators, incomplete baselines or data collected for operations rather than evaluation.
offers.inaction: Without a defensible design and transparent limitations, reports may overclaim, miss decision-relevant findings, fail funder scrutiny or leave future data collection no stronger than before.
for: NGOs, foundations and CSR teams evaluating a programme or portfolio
Policy and implementation teams designing baseline, endline or retrospective studies
Organisations that need independent analysis and reporting with limitations stated
notFor: Teams seeking a predetermined positive conclusion
Organisations unwilling to provide data provenance or disclose material limitations
Requests to manipulate indicators, samples or findings for reporting convenience
outcomes: An evaluation question and design matched to the available evidence
Indicators and analytical choices that can be explained to decision-makers
Integrated quantitative and qualitative findings where the data supports them
A report that distinguishes evidence, inference, limitation and recommendation
deliverables: Theory of change or impact-pathway review
Evaluation framework, questions and indicator matrix
Sampling, instrument and analysis plan
Quantitative analysis, qualitative synthesis and triangulation
Technical report, decision brief and agreed tables or dashboard outputs
inputs: Programme documents, objectives, geography and implementation timeline
Available monitoring, baseline, endline or administrative data
Stakeholder access and ethics/consent status where primary data is proposed
The decisions, reporting requirements and audiences the evaluation must serve

method.title: Frame
method.text: Clarify the decision, evaluation questions, causal logic and feasible level of inference.

method.title: Design
method.text: Set indicators, comparison logic, sampling, instruments and analysis before execution.

method.title: Analyse
method.text: Process and triangulate evidence with reproducible choices and explicit limitations.

method.title: Translate
method.text: Deliver technical findings and a decision-facing narrative without overstating causality.

entryOffer.name: Evaluation Design Diagnostic
entryOffer.scope: A fixed first phase reviewing programme logic, existing data, evaluation questions, indicators and the feasible design before fieldwork or full analysis.
entryOffer.timeline: Typically 1–3 weeks for the diagnostic; a full evaluation commonly runs 6–12 weeks, with fieldwork scale determining the final plan.
entryOffer.investment: Evaluation engagements are typically ₹75,000–₹4,00,000. Multi-site or regulatory-grade CSR impact assessments commonly fall within ₹2,00,000–₹5,00,000.

priceFactors: Number of programmes, sites, respondent groups and languages
Whether primary data collection is required
Data cleaning, linkage and reconstruction effort
Analytical complexity, reporting formats and stakeholder review rounds
exclusions: No predetermined findings or suppression of material limitations
No primary data collection without required ethics and consent arrangements
No causal claim beyond what the design and evidence can support
No transfer of identifiable participant data through insecure channels

faq.q: What if we have no baseline?
faq.a: We first assess whether a credible retrospective or contribution-focused design is possible. The report will state clearly what can and cannot be inferred.
Primary data collection can be scoped where geography, ethics, consent, language and field-management requirements are clear. It is not assumed in the entry diagnostic.

faq.q: Can you work with data we already hold?
faq.a: Yes. We review provenance, structure and limitations before agreeing the analytical claims the data can support.

offers.subject: Impact Evaluation and Analytics

offers.eyebrow: For faculty teams, researchers and doctoral scholars
offers.title: Advanced Research Methods
offers.metaTitle: Advanced Research Methods and Statistical Advisory | Gurjas
offers.metaDescription: Research design, sampling, measurement, SEM, fsQCA, econometric and mixed-methods advisory for faculty teams, researchers and doctoral scholars.
offers.intro: Resolve design, measurement and analytical decisions before they become reviewer comments, failed models or an indefensible methods chapter.
offers.problem: Strong research questions can be weakened by unclear constructs, mismatched methods, underpowered samples, weak measurement or analysis chosen after looking at results.
offers.inaction: Those problems can force expensive redesign, produce unstable findings, delay submission and make the work difficult to defend in peer review or a viva.
for: Faculty and research teams designing an empirical study
Doctoral researchers needing advisory support at proposal or pre-submission stage
Organisations requiring rigorous analysis of an existing dataset
notFor: Anyone seeking ghostwritten theses, papers or fabricated analysis
Requests to select methods only because they produce a preferred result
Work where the researcher will not retain authorship responsibility and understand the method
outcomes: A coherent link from question and theory to data, measurement and analysis
A documented sampling, instrument and model strategy
Clear interpretation boundaries and reproducible analytical outputs
A methods narrative the author can understand and defend
deliverables: Research-design and methodology review
Construct, instrument, sampling and power-analysis plan
Model specification for SEM, fsQCA, econometric or mixed-method designs where appropriate
Output interpretation review with clean tables and figures
Reviewer-response or viva-preparation briefing where included
inputs: Research question, objectives, conceptual framework and current stage
Draft instrument, codebook or dataset where available
Target population, sampling constraints and planned outputs
Any reviewer, supervisor or institutional requirements that materially affect design

method.title: Diagnose
method.text: Test alignment between the question, constructs, data and intended claims.

method.title: Specify
method.text: Document sampling, measurement, model and decision rules before analysis.

method.title: Execute
method.text: Analyse in the agreed package with reproducible outputs and transparent checks.

method.title: Defend
method.text: Translate choices and findings into language the author can explain under scrutiny.

entryOffer.name: Methods Design Review
entryOffer.scope: A fixed first review of the research question, conceptual model, proposed data, sampling, measurement and analysis plan, ending with a written decision memo.
entryOffer.timeline: Typically 1–3 weeks, depending on study stage and completeness of the materials.
entryOffer.investment: Research-design engagements are typically ₹15,000–₹1,00,000; analysis-only work is typically ₹20,000–₹1,50,000. Doctoral proposal-stage support is ₹8,000–₹25,000 and thesis-level review ₹25,000–₹90,000.

priceFactors: Study stage and quality of the existing design or data
Number of constructs, groups, waves or models
Number of review rounds and whether training or viva preparation is included
exclusions: No ghostwriting, fabricated data, invented citations or purchased authorship
No analysis chosen to manufacture significance
No guarantee of publication, approval, grade or degree outcome
No submission of work the author cannot understand and defend

faq.q: Will you write my thesis or paper?
faq.a: No. We provide methodological, analytical and pedagogical support. Authorship and final writing remain with the researcher.
Depending on the design, work may use R, Python, Stata, SPSS/AMOS or SmartPLS, with outputs delivered in the package agreed in scope.

faq.q: Can you review an analysis that is already complete?
faq.a: Yes, provided the data, model decisions and outputs are available. The review may recommend re-specification where the original approach is not defensible.

offers.subject: Advanced Research Methods
